title: Loss and loyalty
summary_text: A famine drives Naomi's family from Bethlehem to Moab, where tragedy leaves Naomi widowed and childless. Ruth refuses to leave her and pledges covenant loyalty, choosing Naomi's people and Naomi's God over her own homeland. The chapter ends with Naomi returning bitterly to Bethlehem just as the barley harvest begins.
key_points: Naomi loses husband and sons in Moab.
Ruth refuses to abandon her mother-in-law.
Ruth confesses loyalty to Naomi's God and people.
Naomi returns home in bitterness.
The harvest signals that hope is near.
evidence_phrases: where you die, I will die
your people shall be my people
your God my God
call me Mara
the barley harvest

title: Ruth gleans in Boaz's field
summary_text: Ruth goes to glean and ends up in the field of Boaz, a kinsman who notices her diligence and protects her. Boaz blesses her for seeking refuge under the LORD's wings, and Ruth returns home with abundant grain. The chapter quietly shows kindness, providence, and the first signs of redemption.
key_points: Ruth gleans to provide for Naomi.
Boaz notices and protects her.
He blesses her faith and hard work.
Extra grain becomes a sign of favor.
The kinsman-redeemer theme begins.
evidence_phrases: her hap was to come
the LORD recompense your work
under whose wings you have come to take refuge
let her glean even among the sheaves
she beat out what she had gleaned

title: The threshing floor request
summary_text: Naomi instructs Ruth to approach Boaz at the threshing floor and ask him to act as redeemer. Ruth's request is modest but bold, and Boaz responds with honor, protection, and a promise to settle the matter according to law. The chapter advances the redemption story while keeping propriety and trust at the center.
key_points: Naomi directs Ruth to seek security in Boaz.
Ruth asks him to spread his garment over her.
Boaz praises her virtue and loyalty.
He agrees to settle the redemption lawfully.
Ruth returns with a promise of rest.
evidence_phrases: spread your skirt over your handmaid
you are a worthy woman
I will do to you all that you say
there is a kinsman nearer than I
sit still, my daughter

title: Redemption and genealogy
summary_text: Boaz publicly redeems the land, marries Ruth, and the elders bless the union with words of fruitfulness and lasting fame. The child born to Ruth becomes the grandfather of David, tying Naomi's empty hands to Israel's future king. The book ends by showing how faithfulness in ordinary loss can serve God's larger purposes.
key_points: Boaz redeems the land before the elders.
Ruth becomes his wife and bears a son.
Naomi receives restored life through Obed.
The genealogy connects the story to David.
God turns loss into royal promise.
evidence_phrases: I have bought all that was Elimelech's
the LORD make the woman that is coming into your house
a son is born to Naomi
he shall be to you a restorer of life
the generations of Perez
